name: Whitefield College
description:
Located in the south of England. Just Northwest of Plymouth and south of Bath is the college town of Ashton upon Avon where Whitefield College, a boarding college for half humans resides. Sat on the Whitefield Quay is the picturesque main building of the college also known as The Minerva Building this building houses most of the main subjects like English, maths teaching, and liberal arts. It also contains the main canteen of the college known as The Corner House.

While another building just behind the Minerva building, facing west, is The Abbey Building this building is a lot older than Minerva being built in 2011 versus Minerva which was built in 2016. Abby building contains more creative subjects like imedia, art, graphic design, advertising, and photography to name a few. It also has the equipment loans desk in there where students can get cameras, drawing tablets, camcorders and any other arty Tech like that on loan for free.

The dorms at Whitefield College are split into three three types the conventional dorms being called Iconic and The Courts, these dorms are open to any species but aren't suited for all, next there are the woodland lodges in the style of Centre parks lodges. These are in the forest that surrounds the back south side of the college. These dorms are suited for Werewolves and Animagus and Centaurs, opposite of Minerva building and on the opposite side of the Whitefield quay are the waterfront lodges. These lodges are suited for Animagus and mermaid students but are open to others

Iconic:

Iconic is One of the conventional style dorms open to most students excluding centaurs due to space, these have been built from old Brown sites as before the college was built. They used to be a part of an old textile factory built in the Victorian era but are now been modernised into Iconic the student dorms.

The Court:

The Courts are the dorms closest to the tennis courts, henceforth their name. They are a new build dorm built recently in 2021, just like iconic they also can house any type of students excluding centaurs.

Woodland Lodges:

The woodland lodges are lodges which are south in the thick evergreen forest on campus. They house students who are more in tune with nature examples being werewolves and centaurs, They are the furthest away from the main campus meaning that it is the hardest to get to. But this does mean that they are a lot more quieter and you can sleep more easily in these ones compared to Iconic or The Courts as they're more in campus

Waterfront Lodges:

The Waterfront Lodges are directly opposite the Minerva building on the Whitefield Quay these storms are open to all types of students excluding centaurs but mermaids take priority over the other students due to its relative locations on the Whitefield Quay.

Ashton Upon Avon:

Ashen upon Avon is the town that Whitefield College resides in. It is a commuter town on the commuter belt with direct links, five trains to London and Plymouth as well as the rest of the country up to Glasgow and Cardiff Ashton Upon Avon is a predominantly richer more upper class town. It does have some chain shops like for example a works, a body shop and a branch of waitrose to name a few. Not too far from the college is the Waterside shopping Centre known for its unique architecture which involves the Canaled section of the river of Avon into its design allowing for people walking on the canal side to enter through the lower floor.

The year system:

Whitefield College uses 4 years to mark students for records

1st Year: 18-19

2nd Year: 19-20

3rd Year: 20-21

4th Year: 21+ (Usually students who are doing Masters or Postgraduate)
